slides.title: Your crash diet is *shedding your hair*.
slides.sub: Sudden hair fall is rarely about your shampoo. It's usually a signal from what you're not eating.

slides.title: Crash diets trigger shedding
slides.body: Very low calories shock hair into a *shedding wave* about 3 months later. Eat enough to hold it.

slides.title: Check your iron stores
slides.body: *Low ferritin* is a common, missed cause of shedding, especially for women. Ask for a blood test.

slides.title: Hair is made of protein
slides.body: Chronically *under-eating protein* weakens new strands. Hit your protein before you buy another serum.

slides.title: Stress pushes hair out
slides.body: Major stress shifts hair into shedding. *Sleep and lower stress* matter more than any oil.

slides.title: Be gentle with strands
slides.body: *Tight styles and hot tools* snap strands you already have. Handle hair gently. Minor, but it adds up.

slides.title: Less shedding, *from within*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the food, sleep and water behind a fuller head of hair — in one daily score.

caption:
Sudden hair fall is rarely your shampoo's fault. A crash diet 3 months ago is a far more likely culprit. 🧬

Eat enough, check your ferritin, hit your protein, lower stress, handle hair gently. If it persists, see a doctor. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks the food, sleep and water behind fuller hair → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Your Crash Diet Is Shedding Your Hair
